title: Why do people hate the Sequel Theory
display_title: Why do people hate the Sequel Theory
wikitext:
[]: Sequel Theory hate             Quote Post by AyrYntake » Sun Dec 07, 2008 3:37 pm NAveryW wrote: People have a tendency to get annoyed whenever someone brings up the idea that the Rebuild series is a sequel to NGE. Certain people, in fact, just can't stand to see people think that Rebuild is a sequel.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Dec 07, 2008 3:39 pm AyrYntake wrote: I think it's because people consider {Neon Genesis Evangelion} or {Neon Genesis Evangelion+EoE} a complete sequence, hence the disagreement at the necessity for a sequel.

[]:             Quote Post by JimmyWolk » Sun Dec 07, 2008 3:43 pm They probably think it reduces it to (bad) fanfic standards, where the "do over"-idea has been used... well, a few times... >_>

[]:             Quote Post by AyrYntake » Sun Dec 07, 2008 3:45 pm It's not really something that can be controlled; a lot of it's to do with gut feeling. As you yourself pointed out, NAveryW wrote: People have a tendency to get annoyed

[]: Re: Thoughts on Sequel Theory Hatred             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Dec 07, 2008 3:46 pm NAveryW wrote: Certain people, in fact, just can't stand to see people think that Rebuild is a sequel.

[]: Re: Thoughts on Sequel Theory Hatred             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Dec 07, 2008 3:53 pm Reichu wrote: You don't need to be a mustelid. You can mention names. :p

[]: Re: Thoughts on Sequel Theory Hatred             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Dec 07, 2008 4:08 pm NAveryW wrote: Indeed, it is quite possible that all of the evidence can be explained another way.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sun Dec 07, 2008 4:55 pm I *openly* decribe Rebuild to people as "dear God, it's a sequel...isn't that crazy-cool?" is it the same as the original series? is it replacing the original series? No; in the sense that the 6 Star Wars movies are one contained "universe", and the Star Wars Expanded Universe of Books and Comics *contains* the original movies, so too, with original and Rebuild: the SW books rely on the original series, but the original series does not rely on the SW books similarly, the original Eva ending works in and of itself, but they made this cool idea for a sequel, post-Third Impact kind of "reboot the world" thing and to think, the biggest original complaint before it came out is that it wouldn't change ENOUGH things and just be a rehash

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Sun Dec 07, 2008 4:59 pm Perhaps it should be called 'Reboot of Evangelion'... Provided some previous mistakes are rectified I don't care if it is or isn't a sequel.

[]:             Quote Post by Rodeo » Sun Dec 07, 2008 5:04 pm My main reason for doubting the sequel theory is the belief that Anno is a somewhat competent storyteller. There was no trace of a neverending story-type element in the original series and it would be a little to late to pull a crazy tweest like that out of your ass now.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Dec 07, 2008 5:12 pm V wrote: I needlessly incorporate unproved pet theories as though they were true when I advertise my digs to others. Isn't that crazy-cool?

[]: Re: Thoughts on Sequel Theory Hatred             Quote Post by JimmyWolk » Sun Dec 07, 2008 5:23 pm Reichu wrote: They might be deliberately trolling, or simply pulling a "made you look" on the old fans. I don't think they've put in anything so far that deliberately creates the suggestion "this story is a direct sequel to a story where very similar events involving similarly-named characters occurred" -- i.e., there's nothing serious or internally-contained about that so-called "immense pile of evidence". Fans of the original show are just seeing some familiar-looking or vaguely 'suggestive' cues and letting their imaginations run wild as a result.

[]: Re: Thoughts on Sequel Theory Hatred             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Dec 07, 2008 5:38 pm Reichu wrote: They might be deliberately trolling, or simply pulling a "made you look" on the old fans.

[]:             Quote Post by AuraTwilight » Sun Dec 07, 2008 5:55 pm Y'all are nuts. [/prequel theory]

[]:             Quote Post by Axell » Sun Dec 07, 2008 7:33 pm of all the evidence given, the first shot of the beach with red water i think is the most important. why put it in at all if its not signifying a link to eoe? also, there was something odd i noticed the last time i watched roe. Right as Shinji hits toji back, that all so memorable drop of water is shown. If i remember right its supposed to signal a change to and from reality right? what could that possibly mean

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Dec 07, 2008 7:47 pm Axell wrote: of all the evidence given, the first shot of the beach with red water i think is the most important. why put it in at all if its not signifying a link to eoe?

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sun Dec 07, 2008 9:36 pm Reichu wrote: V wrote: I needlessly incorporate unproved pet theories as though they were true when I advertise my digs to others. Isn't that crazy-cool?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Dec 07, 2008 10:13 pm V wrote: Fem-Kaworu and Akira?

[]:             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Sun Dec 07, 2008 10:35 pm Reichu wrote: (A) Visual echoing providing artistic/thematic/etc. linkage to the original series. (B) Reiteration/carrying over of an extremely prevalent image from the original series.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Dec 07, 2008 10:59 pm CorporalChaos wrote: Why would this be done if Rebuild is supposed to be a brand new story?

Certain people, in fact, just can't stand to see people think that Rebuild is a sequel.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Sun Dec 07, 2008 3:39 pm AyrYntake w
